user_prompt: read `lib\FlyGui\readme.md` and implement the design, make sure to comment where something is implemented according to a part of my design
agent_reply: - fast vs slow poll mode, with slow mode capped at 15 Hz
- top bar drawing for RTC date/time and battery
- sibling image-buffer lookup to avoid duplicate RAM loads
- partial redraw of changed character ranges
I also added the requested per-screen files under `lib/FlyGuiViews`, one class/file pair per view, starting at [MainScreenView.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.h:5).
I added comments prefixed with `Design:` at the places where code directly implements a part of your readme design.
- A targeted `pio ci` compile did compile the new FlyGui sources cleanly; its final link failed only because the temporary CI input was missing unrelated app demo sources called by `main.cpp`.

user_prompt: `MainScreenView` will consist of 6 items, arranged in as 3 columns by 2 rows, starting from the bottom of the top bar

each of these button sprites are 106 x 144 so they should fit, the top row is supposed to be 10 pixels tall, if it is not, make the adjustment, the battery icon right now is not hugging the top as I expect
The top row are sprit_btn_bluetooth sprit_btn_wifi sprit_btn_memo
The bottom row are sprit_btn_smartphone sprit_btn_laptop sprit_btn_wifihome
Make these as generic clickable FlyGuiItem, each with a generic callback
Create 6 new stub functions in "main.cpp" to wire into these callbacks
Also, BtnA, BtnB, BtnC should correspond to touching the bottom 3 icons. So FlyGuiItem probably needs a function that can call the callbacks from an outside caller
agent_reply: - [MainScreenView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp) now lays out 6 sprite-backed `FlyGuiItem`s in a 3x2 grid starting at `y = FlyGui::topBarHeight()`.
- The generated button sprites are actually `106 x 114`, so with a `10px` top bar the grid fits: `10 + 114 + 114 = 238`.
- [FlyGui.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.h) / [FlyGui.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp) now support generic item callbacks, sprite drawing, and `FlyGuiItem::trigger()`.
- BtnA/BtnB/BtnC now activate the bottom-row items: smartphone, laptop, Wi-Fi home.
- Top bar height is now `10`, so the `20x10` battery sprite hugs the top bar vertically.
- Fixed stale `sprit_btn_home` lookup in [IconLookup.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.cpp) to use `sprit_btn_wifihome`.

commit_message: good progress working on GUI but ran into memory problems with PNG decode

user_prompt: `MainScreenView` creates `smartphoneItem_` and `laptopItem_`, now, I want that if `BtHostList` reports that `getFirstPhone` or `getFirstLaptop` returns null, then the `smartphoneItem_` and `laptopItem_` are created as faded (new feature added recently `void FlyGuiItem::setFaded(bool faded)`) instead of normal
agent_reply: Implemented. `MainScreenView` now fades the smartphone and laptop buttons when `bt_host_list->getFirstPhone()` or `getFirstLaptop()` returns `nullptr`.

I also made it re-check on load/redraw, because the view can be created before the Bluetooth host list is loaded from microSD during boot. That keeps the UI from getting stuck in the wrong faded state.
The first build run timed out while rebuilding a lot of dependencies, but the second run completed successfully. Note: the build regenerated `src/version.c`.
Renamed the six callbacks to `onclick_main_x` in:
I also checked for stale `main_screen_*` references; none remain. I didn’t run a full build for this tiny rename.

user_prompt: the word `wifiHome` needs to become `wifiSearch`. The intention is being changed, original intention was to have a button connect to home Wi-Fi, new intention is to search for any saved SSIDs that are found by scanning
agent_reply: Renamed the main-screen Wi-Fi search button plumbing:
- Log text now says `"main screen wifi search selected"`
- The button now uses `SPRIT_BTN_WIFISEARCH_*` constants with `sprit_btn_wifisearch`
I left the separate `sprit_btn_wifihome` asset / `ICON_HOME` mapping alone, since that still looks like a generic “home” icon concept outside this main-screen button. No full build run.

user_prompt: in `MainScreenView`, trade the top center position for `sprite_btn_info` (currently it is wifi), trade the bottom right for `sprite_btn_wifi` and have it launch the `ScrollView` for Wifi
the info button now goes to a ModalDialog with the `sprite_info_100` logo, and shows some placeholder text. I need to define how to show disk status, unuploaded files status, etc later
remember to edit all the callbacks
agent_reply: - Right hardware button now triggers the Wi-Fi tile.
- Placeholder text is in place for future disk/unuploaded/network status.
- Existing scroll info dialogs still return to `ScrollView`; main info returns to `MainScreenView`.

agent_reply: I added `FlyGui::kTopBarHeight` in [FlyGui.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.h:57), and kept `FlyGui::topBarHeight()` returning that value for compatibility.

commit_message: more gui work, auto bt reconnect

user_prompt: originally I designed buttons to be 106x114 sized because it fit a 3x2 layout
then, the RecordingView was designed and I decided on the thick border and for that some 100x100 sized graphics made more sense
and the modal dialogs and error dialogs use... wild sizes
it's kind of a pain in the ass to manage these different sizes

so I added a tool to automatically resize them from original high resolution art with rules about margins and centering and naming, and want to normalize most of the graphics to be 100x100 with only some exceptions
I have regenerated `inc\sprites.h` and `src\sprites.cpp` while I kept `inc\sprites.h.old` as a reference for the replacements
So the logic in `MainScreenView` needs to account for these changes and keep the graphics in the same locations
Various dialog-like views need their top-center graphics changed to use the 100x100 versions, the text position should follow automatically
There are probably other changes that are required
agent_reply: Changed the UI code to use the regenerated 100x100 symbols across:
- Main screen 100x100 graphics are inset inside the old 106x114 grid so their visual centers stay put.
- Modal/error dialog top graphics now use 100x100 assets, with error text positioned from the sprite height instead of a stale hard-coded old graphic size.
- Connection waiting graphics switched from old 180x150 art to 100x100 centered at the old visual center.
- Removed stale references to deleted `btn_*`, `*_180x150`, `error_large`, `exit_50`, and `handshake_60` symbols.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly` succeeds. The only warnings were existing/library warnings, not from these sprite changes.

user_prompt: but you swapped my memo button with a mic button, that's wrong
also, now that the icons are smaller, it shows that the unload function for the splash screen does not clear the screen, so it shows up through the cracks when mainscreenview is loaded
the same problem is happening when exiting out of scrollview, the old exit door graphic is slipping through the crack
I believe we solved this twice with drawing a gigantic black rectangle in the unload function
agent_reply: The main memo button now uses the proper generated `sprit_memo_100` asset in [MainScreenView.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:49). I copied `memo_100.png` into `gfx-assets/sprites/` and regenerated `inc/sprites.h` / `src/sprites.cpp`, so the symbol exists cleanly.
- [SplashView.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:15) now clears the full screen on unload.

- [ScrollView.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ScrollView/ScrollView.cpp:184) now clears the content area below the top bar on unload, which should stop the old exit-door pixels showing through.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly` succeeded. I also restored the generated `src/version.c` hash afterward, because that file keeps begging us to leave it alone.

agent_reply: Done. The memo click path now fades the main screen and draws `sprit_hourglass_30_overlay` centered on the memo button before `beginMemoRecording()` does its slow startup work.

The feedback drawing lives with the memo button geometry in [MainScreenView.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:94), exposed from [MainScreenView.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.h:17). The memo callback calls it immediately before starting the recording flow in [main.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:422), via the main-view helper in [all_init.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:161).
Build passed. I also restored the generated `src/version.c` hash after the build.

commit_message: recording works great now, tweaked the ui

user_prompt: we are rewriting how the icons work
the idea of a icon now only applies to user customizable things, such as bluetooth hosts, wifi routers, cloud upload destinations.
The user can select between these
This should become the new `ICON_*` list
But, these do not directly map to a sprite.
I have modified `sprite_desc_t`, now any sprite can have an overlay, with enough information for a second call to draw a PNG.
There are three contexts: Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and Cloud
I have modified `IconLookup::getSprite` to take in a context
On the `MainScreenView` bottom row, it should be using `sprite_smartphone_bt_100` and `sprite_laptop_bt_100`, hard code those.
I want the look up to do:
* home = `sprite_home_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bt_50` top right corner
* cat = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cat_50` bottom right corner
* dog = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_dog_50` bottom right corner
* bird = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bird_50` bottom right corner
* circle = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_square_40` bottom right corner
* square = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_circle_40` bottom right corner
* triangle = `sprite_genericdevice_bt_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_triangle_40` bottom right corner
* laptop = `sprite_laptop_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_wifi_50` top right corner
* tablet = `sprite_tablet_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_wifi_50` top right corner
* cat = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cat_50` bottom right corner
* dog = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_dog_50` bottom right corner
* bird = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bird_50` bottom right corner
* circle = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_square_40` bottom right corner
* square = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_circle_40` bottom right corner
* triangle = `sprite_genericwifirouter_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_triangle_40` bottom right corner
* smartphone = `sprite_smartphone_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cloud_50` top right corner
* laptop = `sprite_laptop_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cloud_50` top right corner
* tablet = `sprite_tablet_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cloud_50` top right corner
* home = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_home_50` bottom right corner
* cat = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_cat_50` bottom right corner
* dog = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_dog_50` bottom right corner
* bird = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_bird_50` bottom right corner
* circle = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_square_40` bottom right corner
* square = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_circle_40` bottom right corner
* triangle = `sprite_cloudupload_100` with overlay `sprite_overlay_triangle_40` bottom right corner
agent_reply: The new user-selectable `ICON_*` list is now only: unknown, smartphone, laptop, tablet, home, cat, dog, bird, circle, square, triangle in [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:53).

`IconLookup::getSprite()` is now context-aware in [IconLookup.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.cpp:427), with the Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and Cloud mappings you specified, including overlay placement. I kept the legacy string aliases like `"phone"` mapping to `ICON_SMARTPHONE` so old JSON does not become useless.

`FlyGuiItem` now supports `sprite_desc_t` overlays and draws the overlay as a second PNG call in [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:485). `ScrollView` now uses context lookup for user-configurable entries, while built-in actions like pairing, scan, info, and NTP use hard-coded sprites instead of fake semantic icons.
Main screen bottom-row phone/laptop buttons now use the Bluetooth variants directly in [MainScreenView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:54).

I also bumped the secure NVS config versions and added legacy icon migration so old stored values like old `ICON_WIFI` do not accidentally become one of the new user icons. During build, I hit the existing secure `cloud_item_t` mismatch and restored the cloud password field because the rest of the firmware still depends on it.
The new user-selectable `ICON_*` list is now only: unknown, smartphone, laptop, tablet, home, cat, dog, bird, circle, square, triangle in [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:53).

`IconLookup::getSprite()` is now context-aware in [IconLookup.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.cpp:427), with the Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and Cloud mappings you specified, including overlay placement. I kept the legacy string aliases like `"phone"` mapping to `ICON_SMARTPHONE` so old JSON does not become useless.

`FlyGuiItem` now supports `sprite_desc_t` overlays and draws the overlay as a second PNG call in [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:485). `ScrollView` now uses context lookup for user-configurable entries, while built-in actions like pairing, scan, info, and NTP use hard-coded sprites instead of fake semantic icons.
Main screen bottom-row phone/laptop buttons now use the Bluetooth variants directly in [MainScreenView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:54).

I also bumped the secure NVS config versions and added legacy icon migration so old stored values like old `ICON_WIFI` do not accidentally become one of the new user icons. During build, I hit the existing secure `cloud_item_t` mismatch and restored the cloud password field because the rest of the firmware still depends on it.

user_prompt: read the flow diagram in `developer-docs\ui-design.md` and compare it against the current implementation, there will be things not implemented, and things maybe implemented differently
agent_reply: I compared `developer-docs/ui-design.md` against the current FlyGui/main flow. The short version: the broad skeleton exists, but the implementation has collapsed several diagram nodes into one reusable `ScrollView` plus `ModalDialog`, and the Cloud/Wi-Fi-connected path is much less complete than the diagram implies.
- Boot to Splash to Main exists. Splash stays up until touch: [SplashView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/SplashView.cpp:20>).
- Top bar is global in `FlyGui::drawTopBar`, with date/time, battery, and disk warning: [FlyGui.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:665>).
- Main screen has common actions: Bluetooth, info, memo, phone/laptop quick connect, Wi-Fi: [MainScreenView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:32>).
- Recording has the thick red tally border: [RecordingView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:447>).
- Memo recording exists and can cycle memo type while recording: [RecordingView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:289>).
- Bluetooth connect/pair/info/delete are effectively implemented through the Bluetooth `ScrollView` context and dialogs.
- Wi-Fi AP mode exists as `WifiApModeView`, with credentials and live AP stats.
- Recording stop goes to a finished confirmation dialog, then Main: [RecordingView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:257>).

- `Bluetooth`, `WiFi`, and `Cloud` are not separate concrete screen classes. They are mostly contexts inside one `ScrollView`; the enum still has older-style placeholders like `FLYGUI_VIEW_BLUETOOTH`, `FLYGUI_VIEW_WIFI`, `FLYGUI_VIEW_WEB_ACTION`, etc., but those views are not registered in `init_gui`: [all_init.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:110>).
- `BTConnect` and `BTPair` are both represented by `ConnWaitingView` modes, not separate view classes: [all_init.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:180>).
- `InfoScreen`, `BTInfo`, `WiFiInfo`, `BTErrorDialog`, `WifiErrorDialog`, `CloudErrorDialog`, and finished confirmations are all `ModalDialog` with different text/icons/return views.
- `BTUnpairConfirm` is implemented as ScrollView delete mode plus a modal confirmation, returning to the same Bluetooth scroll context.

- Cloud upload completion always reboots after dismissing the completion/failure dialog, matching the diagram’s success path but also doing it for failure completion: [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:877>).

- `WiFiConnect --> established --> Cloud` is not currently wired. `scanAndConnect()` starts scan/connect, but once connected there is no callback registered to populate/show the Cloud scroll context. `populateCloud()` exists, but I don’t see it called anywhere.
- Selecting an individual Wi-Fi station does nothing right now: `onclick_wifi_station` only logs: [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:638>).
- `Cloud` as a user-visible menu is not reachable from the current flow, despite `ScrollView::populateCloud()` existing.
- `CloudEnroll` and `CloudEnrollConfirmation` are not implemented as GUI flows.
- `PasswordReset` and `MemoryReset` are web/AP administration features, not on-device GUI screens. The AP view intentionally has no stop/reset button except physical reset/power.
- `WiFiAP --> password reset/memory reset --> Boot` is therefore not a FlyGui transition.
- `BTConnect --> cancel --> Main` is slightly different: cancel returns to the remembered previous view, often the Bluetooth scroll, not always Main.
- `BTErrorDialog --> Main` is often `BTErrorDialog --> Scroll`, because connection errors return to the Bluetooth list.
- “Change volume during actual calls” does not appear implemented in the recording UI. There is mic/speaker mode and speaker mute behavior, but not volume adjustment.
- “Mute or unmute mic” is only partially aligned; the UI toggles audio mode and speaker mute, not a clear mic mute/unmute control.

Incoming Bluetooth connection to Recording is substantially implemented through pending flags and `on_bluetooth_state_changed`/recording handling. It applies broadly, with special suppression during pairing confirmation and with Wi-Fi AP activation shutting Bluetooth down, which matches the “except when Wi-Fi radio owns the radio” intent.

The short version: the broad skeleton exists, but the implementation has collapsed several diagram nodes into one reusable `ScrollView` plus `ModalDialog`, and the Cloud/Wi-Fi-connected path is much less complete than the diagram implies.

- Recording stop goes to a finished confirmation dialog, then Main: [RecordingView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:257>).

- `Bluetooth`, `WiFi`, and `Cloud` are not separate concrete screen classes. They are mostly contexts inside one `ScrollView`; the enum still has older-style placeholders like `FLYGUI_VIEW_BLUETOOTH`, `FLYGUI_VIEW_WIFI`, `FLYGUI_VIEW_WEB_ACTION`, etc., but those views are not registered in `init_gui`: [all_init.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:110>).
- `BTConnect` and `BTPair` are both represented by `ConnWaitingView` modes, not separate view classes: [all_init.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:180>).
- `InfoScreen`, `BTInfo`, `WiFiInfo`, `BTErrorDialog`, `WifiErrorDialog`, `CloudErrorDialog`, and finished confirmations are all `ModalDialog` with different text/icons/return views.
- `BTUnpairConfirm` is implemented as ScrollView delete mode plus a modal confirmation, returning to the same Bluetooth scroll context.

- Cloud upload completion always reboots after dismissing the completion/failure dialog, matching the diagram’s success path but also doing it for failure completion: [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:877>).

- `WiFiConnect --> established --> Cloud` is not currently wired. `scanAndConnect()` starts scan/connect, but once connected there is no callback registered to populate/show the Cloud scroll context. `populateCloud()` exists, but I don’t see it called anywhere.
- Selecting an individual Wi-Fi station does nothing right now: `onclick_wifi_station` only logs: [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:638>).

- `CloudEnroll` and `CloudEnrollConfirmation` are not implemented as GUI flows.
- `PasswordReset` and `MemoryReset` are web/AP administration features, not on-device GUI screens. The AP view intentionally has no stop/reset button except physical reset/power.
- `WiFiAP --> password reset/memory reset --> Boot` is therefore not a FlyGui transition.
- `BTConnect --> cancel --> Main` is slightly different: cancel returns to the remembered previous view, often the Bluetooth scroll, not always Main.
- `BTErrorDialog --> Main` is often `BTErrorDialog --> Scroll`, because connection errors return to the Bluetooth list.
- “Change volume during actual calls” does not appear implemented in the recording UI. There is mic/speaker mode and speaker mute behavior, but not volume adjustment.
- “Mute or unmute mic” is only partially aligned; the UI toggles audio mode and speaker mute, not a clear mic mute/unmute control.

Incoming Bluetooth connection to Recording is substantially implemented through pending flags and `on_bluetooth_state_changed`/recording handling. It applies broadly, with special suppression during pairing confirmation and with Wi-Fi AP activation shutting Bluetooth down, which matches the “except when Wi-Fi radio owns the radio” intent.

user_prompt: for anything that is a directly registered callback function from `main.cpp`, move them there
agent_reply: Done. I moved the directly registered callback functions out of `main.cpp` into [src/main_callbacks.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main_callbacks.cpp), with declarations in [inc/main_callbacks.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/main_callbacks.h).

I also updated [src/all_init.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp) and [lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp) to include the new callback header instead of carrying local `extern` declarations. `main.cpp` now keeps the shared flow/helper functions and state that the callbacks use.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed. It only emitted the existing third-party `NetworkServer::available()` deprecation warning.

commit_message: code neatened, remove cloud features with a predefine

user_prompt: add a `ScrollView::populateFiles` function, along with enums to give it a `File List` context
it uses `lib\AudioFileRecorder\MostRecentFiles.cpp`, to populate up to 16 most recent files
it uses `sprite_record_100` for `.wav` files, these are the only type that needs a callback, make the callback a placeholder for now, but later, we will implement a playback function with a view
it uses `sprite_recordenc_100` for `.rec` files, clicking causes no reaction

it uses `sprite_fwupdate` for files matching `firmware.bin`, clicking on it causes the screen to immediately fade (the horizontal stripe routine) and then reboot (which will trigger a firmware update)
it uses `sprite_fileunknown_100` for all other file types, clicking causes no reaction
the file names are displayed as the text associated with the item
also insert a button with `sprite_info_100` for getting a summary of information. This dialog is already implemented. Reuse it
right now, on the `MainScreenView`, the top center item is the information item, switch it out for showing this new `ScrollView` with the file list context, use the icon `sprite_floppy_100`

- storage info item -> `sprite_info_100`, reuses the existing info dialog
- Swapped MainScreen top-center item to the file list using `sprite_floppy_100` in [MainScreenView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:40).
No serious issue jumped out. Note: the sprite generated files/assets were already modified in the worktree; I used the symbols present there and left the unrelated untracked doc alone.

user_prompt: also make sure every `.cpp` file inside `lib\FlyGuiViews` meet the guidelines at `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md`, however, it is also important that their code layout look almost the same between them as they do kind of inherit from the same base classes sometimes
agent_reply: The common shape is now: constants/types/prototypes first, primary view/API methods next, supporting/drawing/formatting helpers later. I also kept the FlyGui view files visually aligned with each other by using the same lightweight section markers and putting helper blocks at the bottom.
- `src\version.c` build churn was restored; final status only shows the intended layout files.

user_prompt: I want to change all `void redraw(bool forced)` to `bool redraw(bool forced)`, and track if anything was actually redrawn from the top level `void FlyGui::poll()` with `bool drawn = redraw(false);`, this bool `drawn` will later be used for a statistic diagnosis
agent_reply: Done. I changed the FlyGui redraw contract to return `bool`, with [FlyGui::poll](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:158) now capturing:

The base `FlyGui`, `FlyGuiView`, and `FlyGuiItem` paths now aggregate whether display work happened, and the relevant overrides in FlyGui text/progress, views, PIN pad, and WAV playback now return `true` when they actually redraw and `false` when they skip.
- `rg` found no remaining `void redraw(bool forced)` under `lib`, `src`, or `inc`.

Only build warning was the existing third-party `ESP-FTP-Server` `NetworkServer::available()` deprecation. PlatformIO also touched `src/version.c` during the builds; I left existing unrelated dirty files alone.

commit_message: diagnostics, fixed a bug with speaker peak decay

user_prompt: in the `MainScreenView`, when the top middle button is being added, choose, based on the current ClockAgent reported second, if it's an even number, use the floppy sprite, if it is an odd number, use the microSD card sprite
agent_reply: Done in [MainScreenView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:3). The files button now checks `Clock.getTime().seconds` right before it is added: even seconds use `sprite_floppy_100`, odd seconds use `sprite_microsdcard`.

Done in [MainScreenView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:3). The files button now checks `Clock.getTime().seconds` right before it is added: even seconds use `sprite_floppy_100`, odd seconds use `sprite_microsdcard`.
Done. I replaced the highlighted logic with `syncFilesIcon()` in [MainScreenView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.cpp:116), using `Clock.getTime().minutes % 6`:
It runs in the constructor before the files button is added, and again in `onLoad()` so the icon can update when returning to the main screen.
